description: This figure presents microstructural and compositional characterization results for three Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels with 15 at.%, 17 at.%, and 19 at.% Al content after respective aging treatment: 15Al aged at 525 °C for 3 h, 17Al and 19Al aged at 525 °C for 10 h. Each sample row includes four characterization outputs: Forescatter Detector (FSD) SEM images showing microstructure morphology, EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) maps for grain orientation analysis, EBSD phase maps distinguishing β-Mn, BCC, α-Mn, and FCC phases, and EDS elemental maps for Fe, Mn, Al, Cu to visualize element partitioning. Observations confirm β-Mn is Mn-rich and depleted in Al and Cu, while higher Al content reduces β-Mn precipitation kinetics: 15Al exhibits near-complete transformation from α matrix to β-Mn after 3 h aging, while 17Al and 19Al only form partial, elongated β-Mn precipitates after 10 h at the same temperature. This supports the core conclusion that increasing Al content suppresses β-Mn phase transformation, which governs the aging hardening response of this alloy system.
